name: new-ups-field-decide-budget-truncation-exemption
tags: ups, context budget, truncation, task_body, execution_contract, new field, checkpoint, dispatcher, task:380140fa
body:
When adding a new field to SessionState that gets rendered into the system prompt (_format_system_prompt), explicitly decide whether it should be subject to _enforce_context_budget (only trims ctx["memories"]) and/or _TASK_BODY_CHAR_CAP (only applies to task_body) — don't assume either applies or doesn't without checking the actual code.

Why: task:380140fa needed a field (execution_contract) that stays byte-identical every turn regardless of context pressure. The risk wasn't _enforce_context_budget (it never touches anything but memories) — it was accidentally conflating the new field with task_body's rendering path and inheriting its char-cap truncation.

How to apply: give a fixed/must-not-truncate field its own section in _format_system_prompt with no shared truncation logic, rather than appending it to an existing capped field. Write a test proving it survives both a large-memories-list budget squeeze and an oversized-body truncation scenario.
